mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, it significantly increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative forward returns for equities over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% for a consecutive 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and declining aggregate demand. This negatively impacts corporate earnings expectations and leads to equity market declines.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns remains positive for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous downturn in both retail sales (consumer) and housing starts (investment) indicates a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand. This joint signal precedes lower corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year growth in both RSAFS and HOUST is negative
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises or accelerating momentum in industrial production reflect robust economic output and corporate activity. This translates directly into higher near-term earnings expectations and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime, further increases in the CPI index create expectations of a more restrictive, 'higher for longer' monetary policy from the central bank. This raises recession risk and compresses equity multiples.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY growth is above 3.5%, the correlation between monthly CPI changes and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth below a critical threshold signals an imminent economic downturn. This causes a flight to safety as investors de-risk portfolios, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of PAYEMS monthly change < 100
failure_condition: The S&P 500 average 20-day forward return is non-negative in the 3 months following the trigger condition being met.

mechanism_narrative: The growth rate of real GDP is the primary driver of aggregate corporate earnings growth. Accelerating real GDP growth implies a stronger earnings environment, supporting higher equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarter-over-quarter real GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.1 over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an extremely low unemployment rate signals persistent wage pressures, forcing the central bank to maintain its restrictive stance. This 'good news is bad news' dynamic negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations.
